member.name: T:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Collections.CollectionExtensions
member.summary: Provides a set of extension methods for common collection types, including , , and . These methods offer additional functionality for efficiently adding or manipulating multiple elements in bulk.
member.remarks:
These extensions simplify common tasks such as adding multiple elements to collections, queues, and stacks. While built-in methods like and only handle single elements, these methods support adding multiple elements at once, improving code readability and reducing verbosity.
The collection types covered by these extensions include:
: Add multiple elements to collections in bulk.
: Enqueue multiple elements at once.
: Push multiple elements onto a stack.
All methods are null-safe and will throw if the target collection or input collection is .

member.typeparam: The type of elements in the collection. If is a reference type, allows nullable elements.
member.param: The to add items to. Can contain elements.
The collection whose elements should be added to the end of the . It can contain elements that are if is a reference type.
member.remarks: This extension is useful for adding range functionality to collections like or that do not have AddRange by default. If is a reference type, explicitly allows values.

member.name: T:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.AnimationComponentExtensions
member.summary: Provides extension methods for the to enhance animation playback functionality.
member.remarks: These extensions simplify the process of controlling animations, such as checking if an animation is already playing before starting it.

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.AnimationComponentExtensions.PlayAnimation(Stride.Engine.AnimationComponent,System.String)
member.summary: Plays the specified animation on the animation component if it is not already playing.
member.param: The on which to play the animation.
The name of the animation to be played.
member.remarks: This method first checks if the animation with the given name is already playing. If it is not, the animation will be started. This approach prevents restarting an animation that is currently running, allowing for smooth transitions between animations.

member.name: T:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.CameraComponentExtensions
member.summary: Provides a set of static methods for working with instances.
member.remarks: This class includes extension methods for performing various operations with instances, such as raycasting, converting screen positions to world positions, and more. These methods are useful for implementing features like object picking, camera control, and coordinate transformations in a 3D environment.

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.CameraComponentExtensions.CalculateRayPlaneIntersectionPoint(Stride.Engine.CameraComponent,Stride.Core.Mathematics.Vector2)
member.summary: Calculates a 2D point where the camera ray intersects the Z=0 plane. Useful for 2D physics engines like Box2D that operate in the XY plane.
member.param: The camera component used for the ray calculation.
The normalized screen position (typically mouse position).
member.returns: A Vector2 representing the intersection point on the Z=0 plane, or null if no intersection.

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.CameraComponentExtensions.CalculateRayFromScreenPosition(Stride.Engine.CameraComponent,Stride.Core.Mathematics.Vector2)
member.summary: Calculates a ray from the camera's position through a specified point on the screen, projecting from screen space into the 3D world space.
member.param: The used to generate the view and projection matrices for the calculation.
The normalized position on the screen (typically the mouse position), with coordinates ranging from (0,0) at the bottom-left to (1,1) at the top-right.
member.returns: A tuple containing two points: nearPoint, which is the world-space position on the near plane, and farPoint, which is the world-space position on the far plane. These two points define the ray from the camera into the 3D world through the specified screen position.
member.remarks: This method is typically used for raycasting and object picking in 3D space, where you need to determine what objects lie under a particular screen-space position, such as the mouse cursor. The ray is defined by transforming the screen position into world space, calculating points on the near and far planes of the camera's view frustum.

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.CameraComponentExtensions.GetPickRay(Stride.Engine.CameraComponent,Stride.Core.Mathematics.Vector2)
member.summary: Calculates a ray from the camera through a specified point on the screen, projecting into the 3D world space.
member.param: The used for the ray calculation.
The position on the screen, typically the mouse position, normalized between (0,0) (bottom-left) and (1,1) (top-right).
member.returns: A starting from the camera and pointing into the 3D world through the specified screen position.
member.remarks: This method is commonly used for object picking or raycasting operations, where interaction with 3D objects is based on screen space coordinates (e.g., mouse cursor). The ray is calculated by transforming the screen position into world space, creating a direction vector from the camera's near plane to its far plane.

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.CameraComponentExtensions.LogicDirectionToWorldDirection(Stride.Engine.CameraComponent,Stride.Core.Mathematics.Vector2)
member.summary: Converts a 2D logical direction into a 3D world direction relative to the camera's orientation.
member.param: The camera component used for the calculation.
The 2D logical direction (e.g., input from a joystick or keyboard), typically normalized between -1 and 1.
member.returns: The corresponding 3D world direction vector.
member.remarks: This method is useful for converting 2D input commands into 3D movements in the world, taking into account the camera's current orientation.

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.CameraComponentExtensions.LogicDirectionToWorldDirection(Stride.Engine.CameraComponent,Stride.Core.Mathematics.Vector2,Stride.Core.Mathematics.Vector3)
member.summary: Converts a 2D logical direction into a 3D world direction relative to the camera's orientation, using a specified up vector.
member.param: The camera component used for the calculation.
The 2D logical direction (e.g., input from a joystick or keyboard), typically normalized between -1 and 1.
The up vector to be used for the calculation, defining the vertical direction in world space.
member.returns: The corresponding 3D world direction vector.
member.remarks: This method is useful for converting 2D input commands into 3D movements in the world, taking into account the camera's current orientation and a custom vertical orientation.

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.CameraComponentExtensions.ScreenPointToRay(Stride.Engine.CameraComponent,Stride.Core.Mathematics.Vector2)
member.summary: Calculates the near and far vectors for a ray that starts at the camera and passes through a given screen point. The ray is in world space, starting at the near plane of the camera and extending through the specified pixel coordinates on the screen.
member.param: The camera component used to calculate the ray.
The screen position (in normalized coordinates) through which the ray passes.
member.returns: A tuple containing the near vector (VectorNear) and the far vector (VectorFar) of the ray in world space.

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.CameraComponentExtensions.ScreenToWorldPoint(Stride.Engine.CameraComponent,Stride.Core.Mathematics.Vector3@)
member.summary: Converts the screen position to a point in world coordinates relative to .
member.param: The camera component used to perform the calculation.

The screen position in normalized X, Y coordinates. Top-left is (0,0), bottom-right is (1,1). Z is in world units from the near camera plane. Passed by reference, allowing for potential optimizations in memory usage.
member.returns: The world position calculated from the screen position.
member.remarks:
This method does not update the or before performing the transformation. If the or its containing has been modified since the last frame, you may need to call the method first.
This method takes the parameter by reference (ref), which may optimize memory usage and prevent unnecessary copies of the vector.

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.CameraComponentExtensions.ScreenToWorldPoint(Stride.Engine.CameraComponent,Stride.Core.Mathematics.Vector3)
member.summary: Converts the screen position to a point in world coordinates relative to .
member.param: The camera component used to perform the calculation.

The screen position in normalized X, Y coordinates. Top-left is (0,0), bottom-right is (1,1). Z is in world units from the near camera plane. Passed by value, which may be simpler to use but less efficient in memory-constrained environments.
member.returns: The world position calculated from the screen position.
member.remarks:
This method does not update the or before performing the transformation. If the or its containing has been modified since the last frame, you may need to call the method first.
This method takes the parameter by value, which is simpler to use but may involve a copy of the vector, which could be less efficient for large or frequent transformations.

member.name: T:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.CameraDefaults
member.summary: Provides default settings for camera initialization within a game or scene, including default positions, rotations, and the main camera name.

member.name: F:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.CameraDefaults.Initial3DPosition
member.summary: Specifies the default initial position for a 3D camera within the game or scene. This position is set as (6, 6, 6) by default, representing the X, Y, and Z coordinates.

member.name: F:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.CameraDefaults.Initial3DRotation
member.summary: Specifies the default initial rotation (in degrees) for a 3D camera within the game or scene. The rotation is set as (45, -30, 0) by default, representing rotations around the Yaw (X), Pitch (Y), and Roll (Z) axes.

member.name: F:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.CameraDefaults.Initial2DPosition
member.summary: Specifies the default initial position for a 2D camera within the game or scene. This position is set as (0, 0, 50), placing the camera far enough from the origin to see the 2D plane.

member.name: F:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.CameraDefaults.Initial2DRotation
member.summary: Specifies the default initial rotation for a 2D camera within the game or scene. This rotation is set as (0, 0, 0) by default, representing no rotation around the X, Y, and Z axes.

member.name: F:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.CameraDefaults.MainCameraName
member.summary: The default name for the main camera used in Stride game projects.

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.EntityExtensions.Add3DCameraController(Stride.Engine.Entity,Stride.CommunityToolkit.Scripts.Utilities.DisplayPosition)
member.summary: Adds an interactive camera script to the specified entity, enabling camera movement and rotation through various input methods.
member.param: The entity to which the interactive camera script will be added.
The position on the screen where the camera controls will be displayed. Defaults to .
member.remarks: The camera entity can be moved using W, A, S, D, Q and E, arrow keys, a gamepad's left stick or by dragging/scaling using multi-touch. Rotation is achieved using the Numpad, the mouse while holding the right mouse button, a gamepad's right stick, or by dragging using single-touch.

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.EntityExtensions.Add2DCameraController(Stride.Engine.Entity)
member.summary: Adds a script to the entity enabling panning and zooming interactions suitable for orthographic 2D scenes.
member.param: The camera entity that will receive the controller script.

The graphics device used for rendering the gizmo.

Optional custom color for the Y-axis of the gizmo. If not specified, a default color is used.

member.name: T:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.EntitySearchExtensions
member.summary: Provides extension methods for searching and retrieving instances within an hierarchy. This class includes methods for performing breadth-first and depth-first searches to find components in children, descendants, and ancestors of an entity.
member.remarks:
The methods in this class support various search options, including:
Searching only in children or including the entity itself.
Including or excluding disabled components.
Retrieving a single component or all components of a specified type.
member.note: All methods throw if the provided entity is null.

member.typeparam: The type of component to retrieve.
member.param: The to search within.
member.returns: The first component of type found in the entity's children, or null if none exists.

member.param: The entity.
member.returns: The component or null if does no exist.

member.name: T:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.EntityTextComponent
member.summary: Represents a text rendering component that can be attached to an entity to display screen-space text.
member.remarks: This component automatically positions text relative to the entity's position in screen space. All color and positional values are managed in RGBA color space and screen pixels respectively.

member.name: P:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.EntityTextComponent.Text
member.summary: Gets or sets the text content to be displayed.
member.value: A non-null string containing the text to render.

member.name: P:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.EntityTextComponent.FontSize
member.summary: Gets or sets the size of the font in points.
member.value: A positive float value representing font size. Default is 18px.

member.name: P:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.EntityTextComponent.Offset
member.summary: Gets or sets the screen-space offset from the entity's position.
member.value: A representing X/Y offset in pixels. Positive values move text right/up, negative left/down.

member.name: P:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.EntityTextComponent.Position
member.summary: Gets or sets an optional position of the text in screen space.

member.name: P:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.EntityTextComponent.TextColor
member.summary: Gets or sets the color of the rendered text.
member.value: A structure defining the text color. Default is for better visibility against dark backgrounds.

member.name: P:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.EntityTextComponent.Alignment
member.summary: Gets or sets the text alignment relative to the entity's position.

member.name: P:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.EntityTextComponent.BackgroundColor
member.summary: Gets or sets the color of the background behind the text.

member.name: P:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.EntityTextComponent.Padding
member.summary: Gets or sets the padding around the text.

member.name: P:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.EntityTextComponent.EnableBackground
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether to use a background behind the text.

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.EntityTextComponent.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.EntityTextComponent.Validate
member.summary: Validates that the component has valid configuration.
member.returns: True if valid; otherwise, false.

member.name: F:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.GameDefaults.Default3DGroundSize

member.summary: The default material color for 3D entities.

member.name: T:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.GameExtensions
member.summary: Provides extension methods for the class to simplify common game setup tasks, such as adding cameras, lights, and ground entities, as well as configuring scenes and running the game with custom logic.

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.GameExtensions.Run(Stride.Engine.Game,Stride.Games.GameContext,System.Action{Stride.Engine.Scene},System.Action{Stride.Engine.Scene,Stride.Games.GameTime})
member.summary: Initializes the game, starts the game loop, and handles game events.
member.remarks: This method performs the following actions: 1. Schedules the root script for execution. 2. Initiates the game loop by calling . 3. Invokes the provided and delegates.
member.param: The Game instance to initialize and run.
Optional GameContext to be used. Defaults to null.
Optional action to execute at the start of the game. Takes the root scene as a parameter.
Optional action to execute during each game loop iteration. Takes the root scene and game time as parameters.

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.GameExtensions.Run(Stride.Engine.Game,Stride.Games.GameContext,System.Action{Stride.Engine.Game},System.Action{Stride.Engine.Game})
member.summary: Initializes the game, starts the game loop, and handles game events.
member.remarks: This method performs the following actions: 1. Schedules the root script for execution. 2. Initiates the game loop by calling . 3. Invokes the provided and delegates.
member.param: The Game instance to initialize and run.
Optional GameContext to be used. Defaults to null.
Optional action to execute at the start of the game. Takes the game as a parameter.
Optional action to execute during each game loop iteration. Takes the game as a parameter.

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.GameExtensions.SetupBase2D(Stride.Engine.Game,System.Nullable{Stride.Core.Mathematics.Color})
member.summary: Configures the game for 2D rendering by setting up the necessary graphics compositor and camera.
member.param: The game instance to configure for 2D rendering.
The color used to clear the screen. Defaults to if not specified.

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.GameExtensions.SetupBase3D(Stride.Engine.Game)
member.summary: Sets up essential components for the game, including a GraphicsCompositor, a camera, and a directional light.
member.remarks:
This method performs the following operations:
Adds a default GraphicsCompositor to the game's SceneSystem and applies a clean UI stage.
Adds a camera to the game.
Adds a directional light to the game.
member.param: The Game instance that will receive the base setup.

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.GameExtensions.AddGraphicsCompositor(Stride.Engine.Game)
member.summary: Adds a default graphics compositor with post-processing effects enabled to the specified game.
member.param: The game to which the graphics compositor will be added. Cannot be null.
member.returns: The newly created with post-processing effects enabled.

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.GameExtensions.Add2DGraphicsCompositor(Stride.Engine.Game,System.Nullable{Stride.Core.Mathematics.Color})
member.summary: Adds a 2D graphics compositor to the specified game, optionally setting a clear color.
member.remarks: This method sets the graphics compositor of the game's scene system to a default 2D configuration without post-processing effects. The clear color can be specified to customize the background color of the rendered scene.
member.param: The game to which the 2D graphics compositor will be added. Cannot be null.
The color used to clear the screen. Defaults to if not specified.
member.returns: The newly created 2D graphics compositor.

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.GameExtensions.CreateFlatMaterial(Stride.Games.IGame,System.Nullable{Stride.Core.Mathematics.Color})
member.summary: Creates a material with flat colors that aren't affected by lighting, ideal for 2D rendering.
member.param: The game instance used to access the graphics device.
The color of the material. Uses white if not specified.
member.returns: A new material instance with flat coloring unaffected by lighting.

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.GameExtensions.Add2DCamera(Stride.Engine.Game,System.String,System.Nullable{Stride.Core.Mathematics.Vector3},System.Nullable{Stride.Core.Mathematics.Vector3})
member.summary: Adds a 2D camera entity to the game's root scene with customizable position and rotation, defaulting to orthographic projection.
member.param: The Game instance to which the camera entity will be added.
Optional name for the camera entity and camera slot. Defaults to "MainCamera" if not provided. If null, the entity will not be named.
Initial position for the camera entity. If not provided, the camera will be positioned at a default 2D position.
Initial rotation for the camera entity specified in degrees. If not provided, the camera will be rotated to the default 2D orientation.
member.returns: The created Entity object representing the 2D camera.
member.remarks: The camera entity will be created with an orthographic projection mode and added to the game's root scene. It will also be assigned to the first available camera slot in the GraphicsCompositor.

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.GameExtensions.Add3DCamera(Stride.Engine.Game,System.String,System.Nullable{Stride.Core.Mathematics.Vector3},System.Nullable{Stride.Core.Mathematics.Vector3},Stride.Engine.Processors.CameraProjectionMode)
member.summary: Adds a 3D camera entity to the game's root scene with customizable position, rotation, projection mode and default camera name "Main".
member.param: The Game instance to which the camera entity will be added.
Optional name for the camera entity and camera slot. Defaults to "MainCamera" if not provided. If null, the entity will not be named.
Initial position for the camera entity. If not provided, the camera will be positioned at a default 3D position (6, 6, 6).
Initial rotation for the camera entity specified in degrees. If not provided, the camera will be rotated to face towards the origin with default angles (Yaw: 45, Pitch: -30, Roll: 0).
The projection mode for the camera (Perspective or Orthographic). Defaults to Perspective.
member.returns: The created Entity object representing the 3D camera.
member.remarks: The camera entity will be created with the specified projection mode and added to the game's root scene. It will also be assigned to the first available camera slot in the GraphicsCompositor.
member.exception: Thrown if the GraphicsCompositor does not have any camera slots defined.

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.GameExtensions.Add2DCameraController(Stride.Engine.Game,System.String)
member.summary: Adds a 2D camera controller to the specified camera entity in the game's current scene.
member.remarks: This method extends the game to simplify attaching a 2D camera controller to a camera entity. The camera entity must exist in the root scene; otherwise, an exception is thrown.
member.param: The game instance containing the scene and camera entities to which the controller will be added.
The name of the camera entity to attach the 2D camera controller to. If not specified, the main camera name is used.
member.returns: The camera entity to which the 2D camera controller was added.
member.exception: Thrown if no camera entity with the specified name exists in the current scene.

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.GameExtensions.Add3DCameraController(Stride.Engine.Game,Stride.CommunityToolkit.Scripts.Utilities.DisplayPosition,System.String)
member.summary: Adds a 3D camera controller to the specified camera entity in the game's current scene.
member.param: The game instance containing the scene and camera entities to which the controller will be added.
The name of the camera entity to attach the 2D camera controller to. If not specified, the main camera name is used.
member.returns: The camera entity to which the 3D camera controller was added.
member.exception: Thrown if no camera entity with the specified name exists in the current scene.

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.GameExtensions.AddDirectionalLight(Stride.Engine.Game,System.String)
member.summary: Adds a directional light entity to the game's root scene with optional customization.
member.param: The Game instance to which the directional light will be added.
Optional name for the new directional light entity. If null, the entity will not be named.
member.returns: The created Entity object representing the directional light.

remarks.para: This method creates a directional light with the following default settings: - Intensity: 20.0f - Position: (0, 2.0f, 0) - Rotation: X-axis rotated by -30 degrees and Y-axis rotated by -180 degrees. - Shadow Enabled: True - Shadow Size: Large - Shadow Filter: PCF (Percentage Closer Filtering) with a filter size of 5x5
The entity will be added to the game's root scene. You can customize the light properties by accessing the returned Entity object.

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.GameExtensions.AddAllDirectionLighting(Stride.Engine.Game,System.Single,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Adds directional lighting from multiple angles to the current scene, enhancing scene illumination.
member.param: The game instance to which the lighting will be added.
The intensity of the light sources.
Specifies whether to display a gizmo for the light in the editor. Default is true.
member.remarks: This method creates six directional lights positioned around a central point, each aiming from a unique angle to simulate uniform lighting from all directions. The lights are added at predefined positions and rotations to cover the scene evenly.

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.GameExtensions.AddGroundGizmo(Stride.Engine.Game,System.Nullable{Stride.Core.Mathematics.Vector3},System.Boolean,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Adds a ground gizmo to the game's root scene, attached to an existing ground entity.
member.param: The instance in which the ground gizmo will be added.
The position of the gizmo in 3D space. If null, the gizmo will be placed at the origin (0, 0, 0).
A boolean indicating whether the axis names (X, Y, Z) should be displayed on the gizmo. Default is false.
A boolean indicating whether the axis names should rotate to always face the camera. Default is true.
member.remarks: The gizmo is added as a child to an existing ground entity. If the ground entity is not found, the method will return without adding the gizmo. The gizmo helps visualize the ground with axis indicators in 3D space.

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.GameExtensions.AddProfiler(Stride.Engine.Game,System.String)
member.summary: Adds a profiler to the game, which can be toggled on/off with Left Shift + Left Ctrl + P, and provides other keyboard shortcuts. Changing the filtering mode with F1, altering the sorting mode with F2, navigating result pages with F3 and F4, and adjusting the refresh interval with the plus and minus keys.
member.param: The game to which the profiler will be added.
Optional name for the entity to which the script will be attached.
member.returns: The entity to which the script was attached.
member.remarks: This extension method creates an entity and attaches a script to it, enabling in-game profiling. The profiler's behaviour can be interacted with using various keyboard shortcuts as described in the class.

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.GameExtensions.CreateMaterial(Stride.Games.IGame,System.Nullable{Stride.Core.Mathematics.Color},System.Single,System.Single)
member.summary: Creates a basic material with optional color, specular reflection, and microsurface smoothness values.
member.param: The game instance used to access the graphics device.
The color of the material. Defaults to null, which will use the _defaultMaterialColor.
The specular reflection factor of the material. Defaults to 1.0f.
The microsurface smoothness value of the material. Defaults to 0.65f.
member.returns: A new material instance with the specified or default attributes.

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.GameExtensions.TakeScreenShot(Stride.Games.IGame,System.String,Stride.Graphics.ImageFileType)
member.summary: Saves a screenshot of the current frame to the specified file path.
member.param: creates a file with this name

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.GameExtensions.AddSceneRenderer(Stride.Engine.Game,Stride.Rendering.Compositing.SceneRendererBase)
member.summary: Adds a scene renderer to the game's .
member.param: The instance to add the renderer to.
The scene renderer to be added, inheriting from .
member.exception: Thrown if the is not set in the game's .

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.GameExtensions.AddRootRenderFeature(Stride.Engine.Game,Stride.Rendering.RootRenderFeature)
member.summary: Adds a root render feature to the game's graphics compositor.
member.param: The game instance to which the render feature will be added. Cannot be null.
The root render feature to add. Cannot be null.

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.GameExtensions.AddParticleRenderer(Stride.Engine.Game)
member.summary: Adds particle rendering capabilities to the specified game.
member.remarks: This method extends the game's graphics compositor by incorporating stages and features necessary for rendering particles. Ensure that the game has a valid scene system before invoking this method.
member.param: The game to which particle rendering stages and features will be added. Cannot be null.

member.name: T:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.GraphicsCompositorHelper2D
member.summary: Provides helper methods for creating and configuring 2D graphics compositors.
member.remarks: This class includes methods to create default configurations for graphics compositors, allowing customization of rendering options such as post-processing effects, model effects, and camera settings.

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.GraphicsCompositorHelper2D.CreateDefault(System.Boolean,System.String,Stride.Engine.CameraComponent,System.Nullable{Stride.Core.Mathematics.Color4},Stride.Rendering.RenderGroupMask)
member.summary: Creates a default configured with specified rendering options.
member.param: If , post-processing effects such as tone mapping are enabled; otherwise, they are disabled.
The name of the effect used for rendering models. Defaults to "StrideForwardShadingEffect".
An optional to be associated with the compositor. If provided, it will be assigned to a camera slot.
The color used to clear the screen. Defaults to if not specified.
Specifies the render group mask to be used for rendering. Defaults to .
member.returns: A instance configured with the specified options, including render stages and features for opaque and transparent objects.

member.name: T:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.ModelComponentExtensions
member.summary: Provides extension methods for the class, enabling additional functionality such as calculating model dimensions and extracting mesh data.

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.ModelComponentExtensions.GetMeshHWL(Stride.Engine.ModelComponent)
member.summary: Calculates the height, width, and length of the model represented by the ModelComponent.
member.param: The ModelComponent whose model dimensions are to be calculated.
member.returns: A Vector3 representing the height, width, and length of the model.
member.remarks: The dimensions are calculated based on the model's bounding box. The returned Vector3 contains the height (Y-axis), width (X-axis), and length (Z-axis) of the bounding box.

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.ModelComponentExtensions.GetMeshHeight(Stride.Engine.ModelComponent)
member.summary: Calculates the height of the model represented by the ModelComponent.
member.param: The ModelComponent whose model height is to be calculated.
member.returns: The height of the model along the Y-axis.
member.remarks: The height is calculated based on the model's bounding box, which encompasses its maximum extent in 3D space.

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.ModelComponentExtensions.GetMeshVerticesAndIndices(Stride.Engine.ModelComponent,Stride.Games.IGame)
member.summary: Retrieves the vertices and indices from the ModelComponent's mesh data.
member.param: The ModelComponent from which to extract mesh data.
The game instance, used to access graphics context for data extraction.
member.returns: A tuple containing two lists: the first list holds the vertices (as ), and the second list holds the indices (as ).
member.remarks: This method extracts raw vertex and index data from the meshes in the provided ModelComponent. It's useful for operations that require direct access to mesh data, such as custom rendering, collision detection, or physics simulations. Note that this method extracts combined vertex and index data from all meshes in the ModelComponent.

member.name: T:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.Primitive2DEntityOptions
member.summary: Option set for creating a 2D primitive entity (quad, rectangle, sprite-like plane) using the toolkit's code‑only helpers.
member.remarks: Inherits common properties from and adds sizing + thickness information suitable for 2D style content that still lives in a 3D world / physics space.

member.name: P:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.Primitive2DEntityOptions.Size
member.summary: Gets or sets the logical width/height for the generated 2D primitive. When null a shape‑appropriate default is chosen by the creation helper (e.g. unit quad).
member.remarks: X = width, Y = height.

member.name: P:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.Primitive2DEntityOptions.Depth
member.summary: Gets or sets the depth (thickness) assigned to the generated 2D primitive. Defaults to 1.
member.remarks: Even "2D" primitives often exist in a 3D simulation. A small Z thickness can improve collision stability or simplify shared 3D physics pipelines; constraints can still lock motion/rotation axes to emulate 2D.

member.name: T:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.Primitive3DEntityOptions
member.summary: Option set for creating a 3D primitive entity (cube, sphere, capsule, plane, etc.).
member.remarks: Extends with a size override. If is not provided, the primitive factory chooses shape‑specific defaults (e.g. unit cube, radius 0.5 sphere).

member.name: P:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.Primitive3DEntityOptions.Size
member.summary: Gets or sets the desired size/dimensions for the 3D primitive model. When null, shape‑specific default dimensions are applied by the creation helper.
member.remarks: Interpretation depends on primitive type (e.g. box uses all components, sphere may derive radius from the largest component, capsules may map Y to height, etc.). Creation helpers document specifics.

member.name: T:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.PrimitiveEntityOptions
member.summary: Base (non-physics) option set used when creating primitive entities (2D or 3D).
member.remarks: This abstraction intentionally contains only rendering / identification related settings. Physics specific configuration is layered in derived option types such as Bepu2DPhysicsOptions, Bepu3DPhysicsOptions, Bullet2DPhysicsOptions, and Bullet3DPhysicsOptions. Additional shared options can be added here as they become engine‑agnostic.

member.name: P:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.PrimitiveEntityOptions.EntityName
member.summary: Gets or sets the (optional) human friendly name for the entity to aid scene inspection or debugging. If null, a name may be assigned by higher level factory helpers or remain empty.

member.name: P:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.PrimitiveEntityOptions.Material
member.summary: Gets or sets the material to apply to the generated primitive model (if it creates a visual component). When null, a default toolkit / engine material is typically assigned by the creation helper.

member.name: P:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.PrimitiveEntityOptions.RenderGroup
member.summary: Gets or sets the render group for the entity. Defaults to . Use render groups to route subsets of entities through specific rendering passes or layers (e.g. separating world geometry, UI routed meshes, debug overlays, etc.).

member.name: T:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.SceneExtensions
member.summary: Provides extension methods for the class to enhance camera-related functionality.

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.SceneExtensions.GetCamera(Stride.Engine.Scene)
member.summary: Retrieves the first found in the scene.
member.param: The in which to search for the camera.
member.returns: The first found in the scene, or null if no camera is present.
member.remarks: This method searches through the scene's entities and returns the first camera it finds. It performs a recursive search through child entities as well.

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.SceneExtensions.GetCamera(Stride.Engine.Scene,System.String)
member.summary: Retrieves the first in the scene that belongs to an entity with the specified name.
member.param: The in which to search for the camera.
The name of the containing the desired camera.
member.returns: The first found with the specified entity name, or null if no matching camera is found.
member.remarks: This method searches through the scene's entities for a camera that belongs to an entity with the given name. It performs a recursive search through child entities as well.

member.name: T:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.ScriptComponentExtensions

member.name: T:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.ScriptSystemExtensions
member.summary: Provides extension methods for the to facilitate time-based operations, including delays and frame-based executions. These extensions are useful for managing time in game logic, such as delaying actions or executing logic over a period of time.

example.para: Example 1: Delaying an action for 2 seconds in game time (affected by time warp):
example.code: await scriptSystem.DelayWarped(2.0f); // Action will be delayed for 2 in-game seconds, accounting for any time warp factors.
example.para: Example 2: Running a continuous action for 5 seconds of real time (unaffected by time warp):
Example 3: Delaying an action for 3 real-time seconds (unaffected by time warp):
example.code: await scriptSystem.Delay(3.0f); // Action will be delayed for exactly 3 real seconds.

member.remarks: These extensions allow you to control how game logic interacts with time, whether you need frame-based operations or time delays. The methods are useful for both real-time and in-game time-based operations.

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.ScriptSystemExtensions.DelayWarped(Stride.Engine.Processors.ScriptSystem,System.Single)
member.summary: Waits for a specified amount of time, adjusting for any time warp factors.
member.param: The instance used to execute the delay.
The number of seconds to delay execution.
member.returns: A representing the delay.
member.remarks: This delay takes into account the game's time warp factor, using the WarpElapsed property of UpdateTime.

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.ScriptSystemExtensions.Delay(Stride.Engine.Processors.ScriptSystem,System.Single)
member.summary: Waits for a specified amount of real time (not accounting for any time warp factors).
member.param: The instance used to execute the delay.
The number of seconds to delay execution.
member.returns: A representing the delay.
member.remarks: This delay operates in real time without considering the game's time warp.

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.ScriptSystemExtensions.ExecuteInWarpedTime(Stride.Engine.Processors.ScriptSystem,System.Single,System.Action{System.Single})
member.summary: Executes an action every frame for a specified duration, adjusting for any time warp factors.
member.param: The instance used to execute the action.
The duration in seconds for which the action will be executed.
The action to perform on each frame, which receives the elapsed time as a parameter.
member.returns: A representing the action execution.
member.remarks: This method accounts for the game's WarpElapsed time factor during execution.

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Engine.ScriptSystemExtensions.ExecuteInTime(Stride.Engine.Processors.ScriptSystem,System.Single,System.Action{System.Single})
member.summary: Executes an action every frame for a specified duration, using real time (not accounting for any time warp factors).
member.param: The instance used to execute the action.
The duration in seconds for which the action will be executed.
The action to perform on each frame, which receives the elapsed time as a parameter.
member.returns: A representing the action execution.
member.remarks: This method operates in real time, without considering the game's time warp.

member.name: T:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Extensions.TextureExtensions
member.summary: Provides extension methods for working with objects. This includes methods for finding asset source directories, resizing textures, and reformatting textures.

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Extensions.TextureExtensions.FindAssetSourceDir(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Retrieves the full filename of the Source of an asset. workDir should be your Assets directory.

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Extensions.TextureExtensions.Resize(Stride.Graphics.Texture,System.Int32,System.Int32,Stride.Graphics.GraphicsContext,Stride.Graphics.PixelFormat)
member.summary: Resizes and reformats a given texture by rendering it to a new texture with the specified dimensions and pixel format. This is useful for processing textures loaded through `Content.Load<Texture>()`, which may be compressed and default to a smaller size like 32x32 pixels. To properly resize a compressed texture, ensure it's decompressed by loading the source file from disk before applying this method.
member.param: The original texture to resize.
The desired width of the resized texture.
The desired height of the resized texture.
The graphics context to use for rendering.
The pixel format for the resized texture (default is PixelFormat.R8G8B8A8_UNorm).
member.returns: A new texture with the specified width, height, and pixel format, or null if the operation fails or the original texture has invalid dimensions.

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Extensions.TextureExtensions.ReFormat(Stride.Graphics.Texture,Stride.Graphics.GraphicsContext,Stride.Graphics.PixelFormat)
member.summary: Reformats the pixels of a given texture via a rendering to texture approach.

member.param: The instance on which to set the FPS limit.
The target FPS rate. Set to 0 for uncapped FPS.

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Games.GameExtensions.SetMaxFPS(Stride.Games.IGame,System.Int32)
member.summary: Sets the maximum frames per second (FPS) rate for the game. Set to 0 for uncapped FPS.
member.param: The instance on which to set the FPS limit.
The target FPS rate. Set to 0 for uncapped FPS.

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Games.GameExtensions.EnableVSync(Stride.Games.IGame)
member.summary: Enables vertical synchronization (VSync) to prevent screen tearing by synchronizing the frame rate with the display's refresh rate.
member.param: The instance on which to enable VSync.

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Games.GameExtensions.DisableVSync(Stride.Games.IGame)
member.summary: Disables vertical synchronization (VSync) to allow for uncapped frame rates, potentially increasing performance at the cost of possible screen tearing.
member.param: The instance on which to disable VSync.

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Games.GameExtensions.Exit(Stride.Games.IGame)
member.summary: Exits the game if it inherits from ; otherwise, throws an exception.
member.param: The game to exit.
member.exception: Thrown if the does not inherit from .

member.name: T:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Graphics.GraphicsDeviceExtensions
member.summary: Provides extension methods for the to simplify common operations related to graphics rendering.

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Graphics.GraphicsDeviceExtensions.GetWindowSize(Stride.Graphics.GraphicsDevice)
member.summary: Retrieves the current window size of the as an structure.
member.param: The for which the window size should be retrieved.
member.returns: An representing the current window size. The value corresponds to the window's width, and the value corresponds to the window's height.
member.remarks: This method provides a convenient way to access the dimensions of the back buffer, which corresponds to the size of the window in which the game or application is running.

member.name: T:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Helpers.VectorHelper
member.summary: Provides helper methods for generating and manipulating objects.
member.remarks: This class contains utility methods designed to assist with common tasks involving vectors, such as generating random values within a specified range.

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Helpers.VectorHelper.RandomVector3(System.Single,System.Single,System.Random)
member.summary: Generates a random Vector3 with each component (X, Y, Z) within the given range. By default, min is 0 and max is required.
member.param: The maximum value for X, Y, and Z components.
The minimum value for X, Y, and Z components. Default is 0.
Optional random instance for custom seeding or performance.
member.returns: A random Vector3.

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Helpers.VectorHelper.RandomVector3(System.Single[],System.Single[],System.Single[],System.Random)
member.summary: Generates a random Vector3 with each component (X, Y, Z) based on the provided ranges.
member.param: Array containing [minX, maxX] values for X component.
Array containing [minY, maxY] values for Y component.
Array containing [minZ, maxZ] values for Z component.
Optional random instance for custom seeding or performance.
member.returns: A random Vector3.

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Helpers.VectorHelper.RandomRange(System.Single,System.Single,System.Random)
member.summary: Helper method to generate a random float between min and max.

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Helpers.VectorHelper.SeedRandom(System.Int32)
member.summary: Optionally re-seeds the default random generator for the application.

member.summary: Performs a linear easing.

member.returns: The amount eased.

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Mathematics.Easing.QuadraticEaseIn(System.Double)
member.remarks: Modeled after the parabola y = x^2

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Mathematics.Easing.QuadraticEaseOut(System.Double)
member.remarks: Modeled after the parabola y = -x^2 + 2x

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Mathematics.Easing.QuadraticEaseInOut(System.Double)
member.remarks: Modeled after the piecewise quadratic y = (1/2)((2x)^2) ; [0, 0.5] y = -(1/2)((2x-1)*(2x-3) - 1) ; [0.5, 1]

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Mathematics.Easing.CubicEaseIn(System.Double)
member.remarks: Modeled after the cubic y = x^3

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Mathematics.Easing.CubicEaseOut(System.Double)
member.remarks: Modeled after the cubic y = (x - 1)^3 + 1

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Mathematics.Easing.CubicEaseInOut(System.Double)
member.remarks: Modeled after the piecewise cubic y = (1/2)((2x)^3) ; [0, 0.5] y = (1/2)((2x-2)^3 + 2) ; [0.5, 1]

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Mathematics.Easing.QuarticEaseIn(System.Double)
member.remarks: Modeled after the quartic x^4

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Mathematics.Easing.QuarticEaseOut(System.Double)
member.remarks: Modeled after the quartic y = 1 - (x - 1)^4

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Mathematics.Easing.QuarticEaseInOut(System.Double)
member.remarks: Modeled after the piecewise quartic y = (1/2)((2x)^4) ; [0, 0.5] y = -(1/2)((2x-2)^4 - 2) ; [0.5, 1]

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Mathematics.Easing.QuinticEaseIn(System.Double)
member.remarks: Modeled after the quintic y = x^5

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Mathematics.Easing.QuinticEaseOut(System.Double)
member.remarks: Modeled after the quintic y = (x - 1)^5 + 1

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Mathematics.Easing.QuinticEaseInOut(System.Double)
member.remarks: Modeled after the piecewise quintic y = (1/2)((2x)^5) ; [0, 0.5] y = (1/2)((2x-2)^5 + 2) ; [0.5, 1]

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Mathematics.Easing.SineEaseIn(System.Double)
member.remarks: Modeled after quarter-cycle of sine wave

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Mathematics.Easing.SineEaseOut(System.Double)
member.remarks: Modeled after quarter-cycle of sine wave (different phase)

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Mathematics.Easing.SineEaseInOut(System.Double)
member.remarks: Modeled after half sine wave

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Mathematics.Easing.CircularEaseIn(System.Double)
member.remarks: Modeled after shifted quadrant IV of unit circle

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Mathematics.Easing.CircularEaseOut(System.Double)
member.remarks: Modeled after shifted quadrant II of unit circle

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Mathematics.Easing.CircularEaseInOut(System.Double)
member.remarks: Modeled after the piecewise circular function y = (1/2)(1 - Math.Sqrt(1 - 4x^2)) ; [0, 0.5] y = (1/2)(Math.Sqrt(-(2x - 3)*(2x - 1)) + 1) ; [0.5, 1]

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Mathematics.Easing.ExponentialEaseIn(System.Double)
member.remarks: Modeled after the exponential function y = 2^(10(x - 1))

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Mathematics.Easing.ExponentialEaseOut(System.Double)
member.remarks: Modeled after the exponential function y = -2^(-10x) + 1

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Mathematics.Easing.ExponentialEaseInOut(System.Double)
member.remarks: Modeled after the piecewise exponential y = (1/2)2^(10(2x - 1)) ; [0,0.5) y = -(1/2)*2^(-10(2x - 1))) + 1 ; [0.5,1]

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Mathematics.Easing.ElasticEaseIn(System.Double)
member.remarks: Modeled after the damped sine wave y = sin(13pi/2*x)*Math.Pow(2, 10 * (x - 1))

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Mathematics.Easing.ElasticEaseOut(System.Double)
member.remarks: Modeled after the damped sine wave y = sin(-13pi/2*(x + 1))*Math.Pow(2, -10x) + 1

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Mathematics.Easing.ElasticEaseInOut(System.Double)
member.remarks: Modeled after the piecewise exponentially-damped sine wave: y = (1/2)*sin(13pi/2*(2*x))*Math.Pow(2, 10 * ((2*x) - 1)) ; [0,0.5] y = (1/2)*(sin(-13pi/2*((2x-1)+1))*Math.Pow(2,-10(2*x-1)) + 2) ; [0.5, 1]

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Mathematics.Easing.BackEaseIn(System.Double)
member.remarks: Modeled after the overshooting cubic y = x^3-x*sin(x*pi)

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Mathematics.Easing.BackEaseOut(System.Double)
member.remarks: Modeled after overshooting cubic y = 1-((1-x)^3-(1-x)*sin((1-x)*pi))

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Mathematics.Easing.BackEaseInOut(System.Double)
member.remarks: Modeled after the piecewise overshooting cubic function: y = (1/2)*((2x)^3-(2x)*sin(2*x*pi)) ; [0, 0.5) y = (1/2)*(1-((1-x)^3-(1-x)*sin((1-x)*pi))+1) ; [0.5, 1]

member.summary: Produces a bouncing motion that starts slowly, accelerates and ends with bounces (ease-in variant).

member.returns: Interpolated value.

member.summary: Produces a bouncing motion that decelerates towards the end (ease-out variant).

member.returns: Interpolated value.

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Mathematics.Easing.BounceEaseInOut(System.Double)
member.summary: Produces a bouncing motion that eases in during the first half and eases out with bounces in the second half.
member.param: Normalized time in range [0,1].
member.returns: Interpolated value.

member.name: T:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Mathematics.RandomExtensions
member.summary: Helper extension methods for tailored for gameplay and procedural content scenarios.
member.remarks:
Provides convenience APIs for:
Generating scalar values () compatible with Stride math types.
Sampling uniformly within 2D rectangles () and 3D bounding boxes ().
Producing random direction vectors in 2D/3D (, ).
Sampling a point uniformly inside a circle () using area-correct square–root radial distribution.
Creating a random opaque color ().
All methods validate the instance and use single-precision math to align with Stride's Vector2, Vector3, and Color types.

member.summary: A custom scene renderer that renders 2D text and an optional background for entities in the scene. Uses for 2D overlay rendering within a 3D scene.
member.remarks: This renderer derives from to integrate into the Stride rendering pipeline. It draws screen-space text for any entity that has an attached. member.name: T:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Renderers.GraphicsCompositorExtensions
member.summary: Provides extension methods for the class to enhance its functionality. These methods allow for the addition of UI stages, scene renderers, and debug render features, as well as utility methods for working with render stages.

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Renderers.GraphicsCompositorExtensions.AddEntityDebugRenderer(Stride.Rendering.Compositing.GraphicsCompositor,Stride.CommunityToolkit.Renderers.EntityDebugSceneRendererOptions)
member.summary: Adds an to the for rendering entity debug information.
member.param: The to which the entity debug renderer will be added.
Optional settings to customize the appearance of the debug renderer. If not provided, default options will be used.
member.returns: The modified instance with the entity debug renderer added.
member.remarks: This method adds a custom to the graphics compositor, allowing the display of debug information such as entity names and positions in a 3D scene. The renderer can be customized using the parameter, which allows the user to define font size, color, and other settings.

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Renderers.GraphicsCompositorExtensions.AddEntityDebugSceneRenderer(Stride.Engine.Game,Stride.CommunityToolkit.Renderers.EntityDebugSceneRendererOptions)
member.summary: Adds an to the game's for rendering entity debug information.
member.param: The instance to which the entity debug renderer will be added.
Optional settings to customize the appearance of the debug renderer. If not provided, default options will be used.
member.exception: Thrown if the is not set in the game's .
member.remarks:
This method adds a custom to the game's graphics compositor, allowing the display of debug information such as entity names and positions in a 3D scene. The renderer can be customized using the parameter, which allows the user to define font size, color, and other settings.
IMPORTANT: This may significantly reduce frame rate. Use only for development or debugging, or in scenes with very few entities.

member.summary: Generates a RenderGroupMask that includes all defined RenderGroups except for Group31. This method dynamically calculates the mask by aggregating all possible RenderGroupMask enum values and then bitwise negating Group31 from the result.

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Rendering.Compositing.GraphicsCompositorExtensions.AddSceneRenderer(Stride.Rendering.Compositing.GraphicsCompositor,Stride.Rendering.Compositing.SceneRendererBase)
member.summary: Adds a new scene renderer to the given GraphicsCompositor's game. If the game is already a collection of scene renderers, the new scene renderer is added to that collection. Otherwise, a new scene renderer collection is created to house both the existing game and the new scene renderer.
member.param: The GraphicsCompositor to which the scene renderer will be added.
The new instance that will be added to the GraphicsCompositor's game.
member.remarks: This method will either add the scene renderer to an existing SceneRendererCollection or create a new one to house both the existing game and the new scene renderer. In either case, the GraphicsCompositor's game will end up with the new scene renderer added.
member.returns: Returns the modified GraphicsCompositor instance, allowing for method chaining.

member.summary: Adds particle rendering stages and features to the specified graphics compositor.

member.exception: Thrown if the "Opaque" or "Transparent" render stage is not found in the graphics compositor.

member.name: T:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Rendering.Gizmos.AxialGizmo
member.summary: Represents a base class for creating an Axial Gizmo, which visually represents 3D axes in the scene. The Gizmo consists of three axes (X, Y, Z), each represented by a different color (Red, Green, Blue). This class provides the foundational functionality for rendering and configuring the visual aspects of the Gizmo.

member.name: P:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Rendering.Gizmos.AxialGizmo.RenderGroup
member.summary: Gets or sets the render group of the Gizmo. This determines which render group the Gizmo will belong to.

member.name: P:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Rendering.Gizmos.AxialGizmo.GraphicsDevice
member.summary: Gets the graphics device used for rendering the Gizmo.

member.name: F:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Rendering.Gizmos.AxialGizmo.GizmoTessellation
member.summary: Defines the tessellation level of the Gizmo, which affects how smoothly the object is rendered. A higher value results in smoother rendering at the cost of performance.

member.name: F:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Rendering.Gizmos.AxialGizmo.GizmoExtremitySize
member.summary: The size of the object placed at the extremities of the Gizmo's axes (e.g., arrowheads or markers).

member.name: F:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Rendering.Gizmos.AxialGizmo.GizmoOriginScale
member.summary: The scale factor of the object placed at the origin of the Gizmo, compared to the objects at the extremities. Typically, the object at the origin is larger to make it more noticeable.

member.name: F:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Rendering.Gizmos.AxialGizmo.GizmoPlaneLength
member.summary: The size of the Gizmo's small planes, which are used for transformations along specific axes or planes.

member.name: F:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Rendering.Gizmos.AxialGizmo.GizmoDefaultSize
member.summary: The default size of the Gizmo when rendered on the screen, measured in pixels. This controls how large the Gizmo appears relative to the screen.

member.name: F:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Rendering.Gizmos.AxialGizmo._redColor
member.summary: Represents the color for the X-axis (Red).

member.summary: Represents the color for the Y-axis (Green).

member.name: F:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Rendering.Gizmos.AxialGizmo._blueColor
member.summary: Represents the color for the Z-axis (Blue).

member.name: P:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Rendering.Gizmos.AxialGizmo.DefaultOriginMaterial
member.summary: Gets the default material for the Gizmo's origin, typically represented in white.

member.summary: Gets the material for the X-axis (Red) of the Gizmo.

member.summary: Gets the material for the Y-axis (Green) of the Gizmo.

member.name: P:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Rendering.Gizmos.AxialGizmo.BlueUniformMaterial
member.summary: Gets the material for the Z-axis (Blue) of the Gizmo.

member.returns: The representing the Gizmo, or null if no entity is created.
member.exception: Thrown if the is not initialized.

member.name: T:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Rendering.ProceduralModels.Procedural2DModelBuilder
member.summary: A helper class for generating 2D procedural models based on a specified primitive model type and size.

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Rendering.ProceduralModels.Procedural2DModelBuilder.Build(Stride.CommunityToolkit.Rendering.ProceduralModels.Primitive2DModelType,System.Nullable{Stride.Core.Mathematics.Vector2},System.Single)
member.summary: Generates a 2D procedural model based on the specified primitive model type, size, and depth.
member.param: The type of 2D primitive model to create (e.g., Circle, Square, Triangle).
The size parameters for the model as a , where X and Y represent the dimensions. If null, default dimensions for the model type will be used.
The depth of the 2D model, which affects its thickness in 3D space.
member.returns: A object representing the generated 2D model. The dimensions of the model will be determined by the provided and , or default dimensions if is null.
member.exception: Thrown when an unsupported is specified.
member.remarks: This method creates different types of 2D procedural models (such as Rectangle, Circle, etc.) with the specified size and depth. The depth adds a third dimension to the 2D shape, turning it into a 3D object (e.g., a 2D rectangle becomes a 3D rectangular prism).

member.name: T:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Rendering.ProceduralModels.Procedural3DModelBuilder
member.summary: A helper class for generating 3D procedural models based on a specified primitive model type and size.

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Rendering.ProceduralModels.Procedural3DModelBuilder.Build(Stride.CommunityToolkit.Rendering.ProceduralModels.PrimitiveModelType,System.Nullable{Stride.Core.Mathematics.Vector3})
member.summary: Generates a 3D procedural model based on the specified primitive model type and size.
member.param: The type of 3D primitive model to create (e.g., Cube, Sphere, Capsule).
The size parameters for the model as a , where X, Y, and Z represent the dimensions. If null, default dimensions for the model type will be used.
member.returns: A object representing the generated 3D model. The dimensions of the model will be determined by the provided or default dimensions if is null.
member.exception: Thrown when an unsupported is specified.
member.remarks: This method allows for the creation of different types of primitive 3D models (such as Cube, Sphere, etc.) with the specified size. If no size is provided, default dimensions for each model type will be used.

member.name: T:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Rendering.ProceduralModels.TriangularPrismProceduralModel
member.summary: Procedural model that generates a triangular prism.
member.remarks: The triangular cross-section lies in the X/Y plane and the prism extends along the Z axis (depth). X specifies the base width of the triangle (across -X..+X), Y specifies the triangle height (across -Y..+Y), and Z specifies the prism depth. The triangle is centered around Y = 0 with the apex at Y = +Y/2 and the base at Y = -Y/2. Texture coordinates are generated per-face using a simple 0..1 quad mapping and scaled by .

member.name: P:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Rendering.ProceduralModels.TriangularPrismProceduralModel.Size
member.summary: Overall extent of the prism.
member.remarks:
X: Base width of the triangle (across -X..+X).
Y: Height of the triangle (apex-to-base, centered around 0).
Z: Depth of the prism (along the Z axis).

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Rendering.ProceduralModels.TriangularPrismProceduralModel.CreatePrimitiveMeshData
member.summary: Builds the mesh data for the current and settings.
member.returns: The generated geometric mesh data.

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Rendering.ProceduralModels.TriangularPrismProceduralModel.New(Stride.Core.Mathematics.Vector3,System.Single,System.Single,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Creates a triangular prism mesh.
member.param: Overall extent where X = base width, Y = triangle height, Z = depth.
Optional U texture tiling scale.
Optional V texture tiling scale.
If true, marks the mesh data as left-handed (indices will be interpreted accordingly).
member.returns: Generated mesh data for a triangular prism (18 vertices, 24 indices).

member.name: F:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Rendering.Utilities.Stretch.Cover
member.summary: The texture is resized to fill the destination dimensions while it preserves its native aspect ratio. If the aspect ratio of the destination rectangle differs from the source, the source texture is clipped to fit in the destination dimensions.

member.name: T:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Rendering.Utilities.TextureCanvas
member.summary: Provides functionality for drawing and manipulating textures on a canvas. This class supports various operations such as drawing rectangles, drawing textures, applying filters, transforming colors, and resampling textures. It is designed to work with the Stride graphics framework and provides methods for loading and storing texture data.

You may consider using if you also want to change the size in a single operation.

member.name: T:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Scripts.Basic2DCameraController
member.summary: Provides an interactive 2D camera controller for navigating 2D scenes in Stride. This controller supports movement in the XY-plane using keyboard inputs (W, A, S, D, arrow keys), zooming in and out with the mouse wheel, and moving the camera based on mouse position near screen edges. Additional features include a speed boost when holding shift and the ability to reset the camera to a default position and zoom level using the 'H' key.
member.remarks: - The camera moves at a default speed which can be increased with shift keys. - Zooming is performed by changing the OrthographicSize of the camera. - The camera can be moved towards the edges of the screen when the mouse cursor is close to them. - The 'H' key resets the camera to its default position and orthographic size. - Default settings: FarClipPlane=1000, NearClipPlane=0.1f, OrthographicSize=10.

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Scripts.Basic2DCameraController.Start
member.summary: Initializes on-screen instruction overlay and caches references.

member.summary: Per-frame update handling movement, zoom and help overlay.

member.summary: Handles F2/F3 toggles for instruction visibility and position.

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Scripts.Basic2DCameraController.ProcessCameraMovement
member.summary: Processes keyboard-driven camera translation.

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Scripts.Basic2DCameraController.ProcessScreenEdgeMovement
member.summary: Moves camera when mouse is near screen edges (RTS-style panning).

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Scripts.Basic2DCameraController.ProcessCameraZoom
member.summary: Adjusts orthographic size using mouse wheel input.

member.name: M:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Scripts.Basic2DCameraController.ResetCameraToDefault
member.summary: Resets camera to default position and orthographic size when 'H' is pressed.

member.name: T:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Scripts.Basic3DCameraController
member.summary: A script that allows to move and rotate an entity through keyboard, mouse and touch input to provide basic camera navigation.
member.remarks:
The entity can be moved using W, A, S, D, Q and E, arrow keys, a gamepad's left stick or dragging/scaling using multi-touch. Rotation is achieved using the Numpad, the mouse while holding the right mouse button, a gamepad's right stick, or dragging using single-touch.
This functionality is inspired by Stride.Assets.Presentation, Assets->Scripts->Camera

member.name: T:Stride.CommunityToolkit.Scripts.GameProfiler
member.summary: Provides in-game profiling functionality, allowing the monitoring and analysis of game performance in real time. This script facilitates the toggling of profiling, setting display preferences, and navigating through profiling data.
member.remarks: This class provides keyboard shortcuts for toggling the profiler on/off with Shift + Ctrl + P, changing the filtering mode with F1, altering the sorting mode with F2, navigating result pages with F3 and F4, and adjusting the refresh interval with the plus and minus keys.
